audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel 4 draft aligned to Ebenezer battle, ark capture, deaths of Hophni and Phinehas, Eli's death, and Ichabod naming; later source/witness audit required.
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Hebrew-first naming, restored/mystical separation, narrative ambiguity, institutional power, vulnerable persons, violence, and misuse risks.

verses.restored: Word of Samuel came to all Israel. Israel went out to meet Philistines for battle and camped beside Ebenezer, while Philistines camped at Aphek.
Philistines arranged themselves to meet Israel. Battle spread, and Israel was struck before Philistines; they struck down in battle line in field about four thousand men.

The people came to camp, and elders of Israel said, Why has YHWH struck us today before Philistines? Let us take to ourselves from Shiloh ark of covenant of YHWH, so it may come among us and deliver us from palm of our enemies.
The people sent to Shiloh and carried from there ark of covenant of YHWH of armies, one sitting above cherubim. The two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were there with ark of covenant of God.
When ark of covenant of YHWH came to camp, all Israel shouted a great shout, and earth resounded.
Philistines heard sound of shout and said, What is sound of this great shout in camp of Hebrews? They learned that ark of YHWH had come to camp.
Philistines were afraid, for they said, A god has come to camp. They said, Woe to us, for there has not been such as this before.
Woe to us! Who will rescue us from hand of these mighty gods? These are gods who struck Egyptians with every blow in wilderness.
Strengthen yourselves and become men, Philistines, lest you serve Hebrews as they served you. Become men and fight.
Philistines fought. Israel was struck, and each fled to his tents. The striking was very great; from Israel thirty thousand foot soldiers fell.
Ark of God was captured, and two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, died.
A man of Benjamin ran from battle line and came to Shiloh on that day, with garments torn and dust upon his head.
He came, and look, Eli was sitting upon seat beside road, watching, because his heart trembled for ark of God. The man came to tell in city, and all city cried out.
Eli heard sound of outcry and said, What is sound of this tumult? The man hurried and came and told Eli.
Eli was ninety-eight years old, and his eyes were fixed, and he was not able to see.
The man said to Eli, I am one who came from battle line, and I fled from battle line today. He said, What happened, my son?
The bearer of news answered and said, Israel fled before Philistines, and also a great striking was among people; also your two sons died, Hophni and Phinehas, and ark of God was captured.
When he mentioned ark of God, Eli fell from upon seat backward beside gate. His neck broke, and he died, for the man was old and heavy. He had judged Israel forty years.

His daughter-in-law, wife of Phinehas, was pregnant, near to giving birth. She heard report that ark of God was captured and that her father-in-law and her husband had died. She bent down and gave birth, because her labor pains turned upon her.
About time of her death, women standing over her said, Do not fear, for you have borne a son. But she did not answer and did not set her heart.
She called boy Ichabod, saying, Glory has gone into exile from Israel, because ark of God was captured and because of her father-in-law and her husband.
She said, Glory has gone into exile from Israel, for ark of God was captured.

translation_notes: The ark is treated by Israel's elders as a battlefield guarantee. Sacred objects, relics, scriptures, flags, and rituals must not be weaponized as magical authorization for war.
Both armies are human communities living under fear, command structures, and mass casualty. 'Philistine' must not become an ethnic slur, timeless enemy category, or pretext for modern hatred.
The Philistines' speech about 'Hebrews' and enslavement reflects wartime identity and fear. It does not define either people essentially.
Large casualty numbers require textual and historical audit and should be read as devastation, not victory metrics.
Hophni and Phinehas die in battle after abusing office; their deaths do not model vigilante justice or prove that every death reveals guilt.

Eli's blindness, age, weight, fall, broken neck, and death must not be mocked or treated as embodied proof of sin. The oracle critiques his leadership and benefit from exploitation, not disability or body size.
Phinehas's wife is unnamed but carries one of the chapter's central interpretations. Her traumatic labor and death must not be eclipsed by male military and priestly narratives.
The women tell her not to fear because she has borne a son. A newborn cannot cancel bereavement, trauma, or dying, and motherhood is not a woman's sole reason to live.
Ichabod is linked with exile/departure of glory. The naming does not make the child cursed or responsible for catastrophe.
The chapter reports ark capture as national-theological crisis but must not erase the many dead, bereaved, displaced, and traumatized people on both sides.
Divine presence cannot be possessed, transported as a weapon, or equated simplistically with institutional success.
safety_note: Do not weaponize sacred objects, glorify casualty numbers, dehumanize Philistines, or read battlefield loss as a measure of spiritual worth. Do not mock disability, age, fatness, traumatic labor, or death. Center Phinehas's wife's voice and the human cost on both sides. This is tragedy, not a template for religious warfare.
